content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
May 11,
1994
Glenn A. Miller
7990 Moss Street
Fair Oaks,
CA
95628
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
I-94-068a
Dear Mr. Miller:
This is in response to your letter dated April 25,
1994,
wherein you ask the Commission for suggestions or clarification
pertaining to the doctrine of incompatible offices.
As discussed in our letter to you dated March 24,
1994,
(our
File No.
1-94—068), the Commission only has jurisdiction over the
Political Reform Act.
In that letter,
you were advised to contact
the Attorney General’s Office with respect to the doctrine of
incompatible offices because that law is outside of our
jurisdiction.
You have indicated that the Attorney General’s
Office will not provide advice to you.
While we cannot advise you on this matter, there are several
published opinions from the Attorney General’s Office pertaining
to the doctrine of incompatible offices.
You may wish to consult
the published Attorney General opinions at your local law library.
In addition,
I have taken the liberty of enclosing some general
information.
I believe Chapter
12 discusses the areas in which
you are interested.
You may also want to consider contacting the attorney
representing the district (this may be the city attorney or county
counsel)
for specific advice pertaining to your situation.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322-5901.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Lynda Doherty
Politica]Rform Consultant
Legal Division
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked for suggestions or clarification regarding the doctrine of incompatible offices, following up on a previous FPPC letter that redirected the inquiry to the Attorney General's Office.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC reaffirms that the doctrine of incompatible offices is outside its jurisdiction under the Political Reform Act and recommends that the requester consult published Attorney General opinions, contact their local law library, or seek advice from the district's attorney (city attorney or county counsel).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a follow-up response to an earlier inquiry (File No. I-94-068). No formal Q&A sections present; the question and conclusion are embedded in the letter text. The FPPC clearly states it lacks jurisdiction over the subject matter and directs the requester to external resources.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester asked for suggestions or clarification regarding the doctrine of incompatible offices, following up on a previous FPPC letter that redirected the inquiry to the Attorney General's Office.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC reaffirms that the doctrine of incompatible offices is outside its jurisdiction under the Political Reform Act and recommends that the requester consult published Attorney General opinions, contact their local law library, or seek advice from the district's attorney (city attorney or county counsel).
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission May 11, 1994 Glenn A. Miller 7990 Moss Street Fair Oaks, CA 95628 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our File No. I-94-068a Dear Mr. Miller: This is in response to your letter dated April 25, 1994, wherein you ask the Commission for suggestions or clarification pertaining to the doctrine of incompatible offices. As discussed in our letter to you dated March 24, 1994, (our File No. 1-94—068), the Commission only has jurisdiction over the Political Reform Act. In that letter, you were advised to contact the Attorney General’s Office with respect to the doctrine of incompatible offices because that law is outside of our jurisdiction. You have indicated that the Attorney General’s Office will not provide advice to you. While we cannot advise you on this matter, there are several published opinions from the Attorney General’s Office pertaining to the doctrine of incompatible offices. You may wish to consult the published Attorney General opinions at your local law library. In addition, I have taken the liberty of enclosing some general information. I believe Chapter 12 discusses the areas in which you are interested. You may also want to consider contacting the attorney representing the district (this may be the city attorney or county counsel) for specific advice pertaining to your situation. If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901. Sincerely, Steven G. Churchwell General Counsel By: Lynda Doherty Politica]Rform Consultant Legal Division 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: This is an FPPC informal advice letter declining jurisdiction over a question about the doctrine of incompatible offices, reiterating a previous referral to the Attorney General's Office and providing alternative resources for the requester.
